stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 20 observations with mean 78.5 % (within-trial site SD = 3.4). trial site B has 10 observations with mean 68.4 % (within-trial site SD = 5.4). trial site C has 5 observations with mean 84.0 % (within-trial site SD = 3.5). trial site D has 8 observations with mean 98.6 % (within-trial site SD = 5.4). trial site E has 5 observations with mean 64.0 % (within-trial site SD = 8.3). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.5 and 13.8 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site B: 68.7 % (shrunk from raw 68.4). Range [68.6, 69.3] as between-trial site SD varies from 5.5 to 13.8. Pooled mean: 78.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 78.8 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=9.2: B = 0.967.
Interval [68.6, 69.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (68.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (78.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
